message:
fix(ssh): use ~/.ssh/rch for control sockets to fix macOS 104-byte limit

macOS enforces a 104-byte limit on Unix domain socket paths. The
openssh crate appends a %C hash (~32 chars) to the control directory
to form socket filenames. The previous path under $TMPDIR
(/var/folders/.../T/rch-ssh-{user}/) produced 106+ character paths,
causing every SSH connection to silently fail with RCH-E100.

Switch both SshClient and rsync_control_dir to prefer ~/.ssh/rch/
(~25 chars) which keeps total socket paths well under the limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
